decks.notes: The deck focuses on quickly assembling the combo of Cephalid Illusionist and Nomads en-Kor to mill your entire library. This allows you to then use Reanimate effects to bring back a massive creature like Sutured Ghoul or Terravore for a game-winning attack. Using cards like Cabal Therapy and Duress, the deck aims to disrupt your opponent's strategy while you set up your own. The deck is designed to be fast and explosive, aiming to win the game before your opponent has a chance to respond.

The main concept of this deck is to control the game by using counterspells and removal spells to disrupt the opponent's strategy. The deck also focuses on land denial with cards like Parallax Tide and Dust Bowl, while maintaining card advantage with Fact or Fiction. The win condition is typically through creating a board advantage with Mishra's Factory and Faerie Conclave, or overwhelming the opponent with Decree of Justice. Standstill is used to slow down the game and generate value for the control player. The deck aims to outlast the opponent and win through incremental advantages.

The deck is focused on utilizing the mana acceleration provided by cards such as Fyndhorn Elves and Llanowar Elves to quickly ramp up and flood the board with Elf creatures. By efficiently utilizing Quirion Ranger and Wirewood Symbiote to untap mana dorks and generating additional mana through Priest of Titania, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a swarm of Elves. Additionally, the inclusion of cards like Deranged Hermit and Multani's Acolyte provide additional value and resilience to the deck while Survival of the Fittest acts as a toolbox for tutoring key creatures.

The primary focus of this Mono Black Control deck is to disrupt and control the opponent's resources while generating card advantage and eventually overwhelming them with efficient threats. By utilizing a combination of hand disruption spells like Duress and Cabal Therapy, removal spells such as Innocent Blood and Smother, along with engine cards like Zombie Infestation and Contamination, the deck aims to control the flow of the game and grind out incremental advantages over time. Additionally, the deck can use land destruction from Wasteland and Rishadan Port to further hinder the opponent's ability to develop their board.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly overwhelm your opponent with a swarm of small creatures and pump them up with spells like Shared Triumph and Coat of Arms. The deck also uses cards like Goblin Bombardment for direct damage and removal spells like Swords to Plowshares. With cards like Mother of Runes providing protection and support, this aggro deck aims to win the game quickly and decisively by using the synergy between its various creatures and spells.

The main focus of this deck is to put pressure on the opponent quickly with aggressive creatures like Carnophage and Skittering Skirge, while disrupting their game plan with cards like Duress and Withered Wretch. Winter Orb and Standstill help to slow down the opponent's mana development and card advantage, allowing the deck to maintain its tempo. The deck's mana base is optimized for casting spells quickly and efficiently, ensuring that the aggressive strategy can be executed effectively.

This deck focuses on quickly deploying efficient creatures with the Madness ability, allowing for cheap spells to be cast during the opponent's turn. The key strategy is to use Survival of the Fittest to tutor for the right creatures, while also disrupting the opponent's game plan with countermagic and removal spells. The deck aims to apply early pressure and maintain board presence through creature recursion and card advantage, ultimately overwhelming the opponent before they can stabilize. The mana base is streamlined to support the deck's aggressive game plan.

The deck focuses on utilizing card draw, counterspells, and removal spells to control the game while building up the power of Psychatog. By efficiently managing resources and disrupting opponent's game plan, the deck aims to outvalue and outmaneuver opponents. The key to success lies in effectively using card draw spells like Accumulated Knowledge and Impulse to find answers, protecting Psychatog with counterspells like Counterspell, and removing threats with spells like Smother. The goal is to overwhelm opponents with a powerful Psychatog while maintaining control over the game.

This deck focuses on using the combo potential of Aluren to cast creatures for free and generate card advantage. By combining Aluren with creatures like Wall of Roots and Raven Familiar, players can generate infinite card draw. Cards like Man-o'-War and Spike Feeder provide additional utility and combo pieces. The deck also includes disruption in the form of Meddling Mage and removal with Swords to Plowshares. Survival of the Fittest helps to tutor for combo pieces, while Sylvan Library provides additional card selection. The mana base includes a mix of lands to ensure consistent mana production.

The main idea behind this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and aggressive playstyle using Goblins. The key strategy revolves around playing low-cost creatures like Goblin Lackey and Mogg Fanatic to quickly build up a board presence, while using cards like Goblin Matron and Goblin Ringleader to search for more Goblins and maintain card advantage. With cards like Goblin Warchief and Siege-Gang Commander to pump up the Goblins, and removal spells like Gempalm Incinerator and Naturalize for control, the deck aims to swiftly defeat the opponent before they have a chance to set up their own game plan.

The main concept of the deck is to overwhelm the opponent quickly with a combination of aggressive creatures and direct damage spells. By applying early pressure with creatures like Hypnotic Specter and Ravenous Rats, followed up by burn spells like Lightning Bolt, the deck aims to control the pace of the game and disrupt the opponent's strategy. This aggressive approach is supported by disruption cards like Duress and Cabal Therapy to disrupt the opponent's hand and keep them on the back foot. Overall, the deck is designed to be fast and punishing, aiming to close out games quickly.

The Mono Black Aggro deck focuses on quickly overwhelming the opponent with efficient creatures, disruption through hand disruption spells like Duress and Cabal Therapy, and removal spells like Snuff Out and Smother. The deck also utilizes card advantage from cards like Phyrexian Arena and Graveborn Muse to maintain pressure on the opponent. Additionally, it has a land destruction package with Wasteland to disrupt the opponent's mana base. Overall, the deck aims to apply consistent pressure while stripping the opponent's resources to secure a victory.

This deck focuses on utilizing the combo of Replenish and various enchantments like Parallax Wave and Opalescence to control the board and overwhelm the opponent. By constantly recycling enchantments from the graveyard and triggering their effects, the deck aims to create a powerful and synergistic engine that can quickly swing the game in its favor. The deck also includes a mix of counterspells and removal to protect its combo pieces and disrupt the opponent's strategy. It is a versatile and intricate deck that requires careful planning and timing to execute successfully.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly assemble the Phyrexian Dreadnought combo by playing a 12/12 creature for one mana with the help of Stifle. The deck also includes various card draw spells and counterspells to protect the combo and control the game. Additional utility cards like Chain of Vapor and Mishra's Factory provide flexibility in different matchups. The goal is to disrupt the opponent's game plan while setting up your combo to win the game.

The deck revolves around quickly dropping a Phyrexian Dreadnought and using Stifle to counter any triggers that would normally remove it from the battlefield. This allows for a powerful, game-winning combo to be set up. The deck also includes card draw and counterspells to control the game and protect the combo. The goal is to efficiently stall the opponent's game plan while setting up the combo for a quick victory.

This deck focuses on playing aggressive creatures such as Nimble Mongoose, Terravore, and Werebear, while disrupting the opponent's mana base with cards like Armageddon and Wasteland. Weathered Wayfarer helps find key lands while Sylvan Library provides card advantage. Using Mox Diamond for fast mana acceleration, the deck aims to control the board with removal spells like Swords to Plowshares and Vindicate. The inclusion of Gerrard's Verdict helps disrupt the opponent's hand. The deck is designed to quickly apply pressure and control the game through aggression and land destruction.
